unit.name: Zero-shot and few-shot in-context learning
unit.description: Explains prompting as task specification without parameter updates.
unit.summary: The lecture introduces GPT-style decoder-only models trained with next-token prediction, then uses GPT-2 and GPT-3 to explain zero-shot and few-shot learning [ts=442s] [ts=461s]. Zero-shot means the model is shown no examples but can still summarize, follow instructions, or solve simple tasks because pretraining has exposed it to many patterns [ts=532s] [ts=558s]. Prompting works by coercing an autocomplete model: place context and a question in the prompt, or compare candidate completions by log probability, as in the cat/hat coreference example [ts=584s] [ts=619s]. A TLDR suffix can induce zero-shot summarization because that pattern appears in internet text [ts=688s] [ts=707s]. GPT-3-style few-shot learning places examples in context with no gradient updates; the model infers the task pattern and can approach strong task-specific systems [ts=846s] [ts=920s].

key_points.text: Zero-shot learning is task performance with no task-specific examples at inference time.
Prompting is described as coercing an autocomplete model into completing the task you care about.
Log probabilities can score candidate answers, illustrated by resolving whether 'it' refers to the cat or hat.
Few-shot in-context learning uses examples in the prompt with no gradient updates.
In-context learning improves with scale, although the appearance of emergence is partly contested.

unit_summary_for_generation: The lecture introduces GPT-style decoder-only models trained with next-token prediction, then uses GPT-2 and GPT-3 to explain zero-shot and few-shot learning [ts=442s] [ts=461s]. Zero-shot means the model is shown no examples but can still summarize, follow instructions, or solve simple tasks because pretraining has exposed it to many patterns [ts=532s] [ts=558s]. Prompting works by coercing an autocomplete model: place context and a question in the prompt, or compare candidate completions by log probability, as in the cat/hat coreference example [ts=584s] [ts=619s]. A TLDR suffix can induce zero-shot summarization because that pattern appears in internet text [ts=688s] [ts=707s]. GPT-3-style few-shot learning places examples in context with no gradient updates; the model infers the task pattern and can approach strong task-specific systems [ts=846s] [ts=920s].

kp_catalog.name: In-context learning
kp_catalog.description: Solving a task from examples placed directly in the prompt, without updating model parameters. Task behavior induced by prompts and examples without parameter updates.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because in-context learning clarifies solving a task from examples placed directly in the prompt, without updating model parameters, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices though roles split between capstone and supporting.

kp_catalog.name: Pretraining scaling and compute-data tradeoffs
kp_catalog.description: Large-scale pretraining quality depends jointly on model size, training data, and compute budget, with capabilities improving as those resources scale. This scaling view also appears in vision-language systems such as CLIP, where larger models and more image-text data improve performance.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because pretraining scaling and compute-data tradeoffs clarifies large-scale pretraining quality depends jointly on model size, training data, and compute budget, with capabilities improving as those resources scale, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices while importance labels split between high and medium.
